# CDI Data Acquisition System Overview

The CDI Data Acquisition System consists of a series of interconnected systems that transform public omics data into reusable reference datasets.

```text
Study Discovery
        ↓
Metadata Acquisition
        ↓
Data Download
        ↓
Data Validation
        ↓
Cloud Storage and Transfer
        ↓
Reference Dataset Assembly
```

## Repository Accession Reference

Common accession types encountered during data acquisition:

| Resource | Example |
|-----------|-----------|
| BioProject | PRJNA477349 |
| BioSample | SAMN12345678 |
| SRA Run | SRR12345678 |
| GEO Series | GSE123456 |
| ENA Study | ERP123456 |

## Directory Structure Reference

Example CDI Data Acquisition System project structure:

```text
project/
├── data/
│   ├── metadata/
│   ├── manifests/
│   ├── inventory/
│   ├── raw/
│   └── validation/
├── scripts/
│   └── bash/
├── library/
├── docs/
└── environment.yml
```

## Bash Scripts Reference

The following scripts are developed throughout this guide.

```text
scripts/bash/
├── 05a-build-download-manifest.sh
├── 05b-download-ena-fastq.sh
├── 05c-download-ncbi-sra.sh
├── 05d-verify-downloads.sh
├── 05e-build-fastq-inventory.sh
├── 06-validate-download.sh
└── 07-transfer-to-cloud.sh
```

## Common Repository Tools

### Metadata Acquisition

```text
EDirect
pysradb
curl
wget
```

### Data Download

```text
SRA Toolkit
wget
curl
```

### Validation

```text
gzip
seqkit
csvtk
```

### Cloud Storage

```text
AWS CLI
rsync
Aspera
```

## Conda Environment

Example environment:

```bash
conda env create -f environment.yml

conda activate cdi-data-acquisition
```

## Recommended Workflow

```text
Research Question
        ↓
Study Discovery
        ↓
Metadata Acquisition
        ↓
Metadata Review
        ↓
Data Download
        ↓
Data Validation
        ↓
Cloud Storage
        ↓
Reference Dataset Assembly
        ↓
Dataset Release
```

## Key Principles

The CDI Data Acquisition System is built upon the following principles:

- Reproducibility
- Transparency
- Metadata-first design
- Validation before analysis
- Provenance preservation
- Cloud-ready workflows
- Reusable reference datasets

## Bibliography and Resources

The references cited throughout this guide are maintained in the bibliography database (`library/references.bib`) and are included automatically during book generation.

This appendix also provides a quick reference to commonly cited resources and repositories used throughout the CDI Data Acquisition System.

## Suggested Citation Keys

The following citation keys are commonly used throughout the CDI Data Acquisition System:

- [@Leinonen2011]
- [@Kodama2012]
- [@Barrett2013]
- [@Arita2021]
- [@Brazma2001]
- [@Yilmaz2011]
- [@Wilkinson2016]
- [@Sandve2013]
- [@Mitchell2020]
- [@Knight2018]

## Major Public Data Resources

- **NCBI** — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov [@NCBI]
- **Sequence Read Archive (SRA)** — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/sra [@SRA]
- **BioProject** — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/bioproject [@BioProject]
- **BioSample** — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/biosample [@BioSample]
- **Gene Expression Omnibus (GEO)** — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo [@GEO]
- **European Nucleotide Archive (ENA)** — https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ena [@ENA]
- **MGnify** — https://www.ebi.ac.uk/metagenomics [@MGnify]
- **GWAS Catalog** — https://www.ebi.ac.uk/gwas [@GWASCatalog]
- **DNA Data Bank of Japan (DDBJ)** — https://www.ddbj.nig.ac.jp [@DDBJ]
